title: Currywurst

overview.description: A German street food classic of grilled pork sausage served with a homemade curried tomato sauce. Simple, comforting and perfect with crispy chips or fresh bread.

ingredients: 8 bratwurst or good quality pork sausages
method: Heat the vegetable oil in a saucepan over a medium heat.
Add the onion and cook gently for about 8 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the curry powder, smoked paprika and tomato purée and cook for 30 seconds.
Add the chopped tomatoes, Worcestershire sauce, apple cider vinegar and sugar.
Simmer gently for 20 minutes until thickened.
Blend until smooth if you prefer a traditional silky sauce, or leave slightly chunky.
Meanwhile, grill or pan-fry the sausages until cooked through and nicely browned.
Slice the sausages into bite-sized pieces.
Serve generously covered with the curry sauce and dust with a little extra curry powder.

learning.did-you-know: Currywurst was invented in Berlin in 1949 and has become one of Germany's most famous street foods, with millions sold every year.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build a simple curry ketchup from scratch and how gently cooking spices develops a richer flavour.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A fun Friday meal that's quick to prepare and introduces German cooking without requiring specialist techniques.
chapman-tips: Use the best sausages you can find—they're the star of the dish.
The sauce tastes even better after standing for half an hour.
Blend the sauce for a more authentic Berlin-style finish.
notes.batch-cooking: The sauce freezes very well. Cook fresh sausages when serving.
notes.leftovers: Reheat the sauce gently and grill fresh sausages if possible.
